response:
The birth name of the disc jockey who notably used Mark Wirtz's song "A Touch of Velvet, A Sting of Brass" on their Radio Caroline show is **Tony Blackburn**. 

This is evidenced by the context, which states:  
> "Tony Blackburn: Antony Kenneth 'Tony' Blackburn (born 29 January 1943) is an English disc jockey who broadcast on the 'pirate' stations Radio Caroline and Radio London in the 1960s and was the second disc jockey to broadcast on BBC Radio 1 at its launch at the end of September 1967."
